retrospective.harder_than_needed: The spec/architecture/verification.json trio for this story was unusually complete (exact byte offsets, exact flag bits, the AC-SIG-6 id-attachment recipe already spelled out with the file:line precedent in signerAdapter.ts) — implementation was close to mechanical translation. The only real friction was self-inflicted tooling noise: while iterating on one test (the name_len=0/30 length-check generalization test), an Edit call's replacement text landed in the file with a literal space character silently turned into a NUL byte (`'\x00'.repeat(0)` instead of `' '.repeat(0)`), which then made subsequent exact-string Edit matches fail with no diagnostic beyond "string not found" — diagnosing it required dumping the file through Python with repr() to see the actual bytes. Not clearly a harness bug (may have been an artifact of how a single-space string literal moved through this tool call), but worth a note since it cost a few minutes of confused re-matching before the repr() dump made the corruption visible.
